     The Numbers Business Report

Late Night (2019)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $15,500,045Details
International Box Office $6,873,333
Worldwide Box Office $22,373,378
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Katherine Newbury is a pioneer and legendary host on the late-night talk-show circuit. When she’s accused of being a “woman who hates women,” she puts affirmative action on the to-do list, and—presto!—Molly is hired as the one woman in Katherine’s all-male writers’ room. But Molly might be too little too late, as the formidable Katherine also faces the reality of low ratings and a network that wants to replace her. Molly, wanting to prove she’s not simply a diversity hire who’s disrupting the comfort of the brotherhood, is determined to help Katherine by revitalizing her show and career—and possibly effect even bigger change at the same time.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$246,305 (1.6% of total gross)
Legs:2.95 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:69.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$4,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/2,220 max. theaters, 2.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $20,238,054

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 7th, 2019 (Limited) by Amazon Studios
June 14th, 2019 (Expands Wide) by Amazon Studios
International Releases: June 7th, 2019 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
July 12th, 2019 (Wide) (Czech Republic)
August 1st, 2019 (Wide) (Malaysia)
August 8th, 2019 (Wide) (New Zealand)
August 9th, 2019 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: September 6th, 2019 by Prime Video
MPA Rating: R for language throughout and some sexual references.
(Rating bulletin 2571 (Cert #51996), 3/27/2019)
Running Time: 102 minutes
Film festival: Sundance Film Festival 2019
Plot point: Woman in a Male Dominated Profession, Writing and Writers
Social setting: TV Industry
Subgenre: Comedy Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: FilmNation Entertainment, 3 Arts Entertainment, Imperative Entertainment, 30 West, Amazon Studios, Kaling International
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Katherine Newbury is a pioneer and legendary host on the late-night talk-show circuit. When she’s accused of being a “woman who hates women,” she puts affirmative action on the to-do list, and—presto!—Molly is hired as the one woman in Katherine’s all-male writers’ room. But Molly might be too little too late, as the formidable Katherine also faces the reality of low ratings and a network that wants to replace her. Molly, wanting to prove she’s not simply a diversity hire who’s disrupting the comfort of the brotherhood, is determined to help Katherine by revitalizing her show and career—and possibly effect even bigger change at the same time.
